title: Centropogon amplicorollinus
taxonomy: Centropogon amplicorollinus is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. This plant is native to tropical regions, specifically in Central and South America. Further classification of Centropogon amplicorollinus is within the genus Centropogon. It belongs to the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. The plant is an herbaceous perennial with small, bell-shaped flowers.

constituents: Unfortunately, I do not have information on the active constituents of Centropogon amplicorollinus.
intro: Centropogon amplicorollinus is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae, commonly known as a wild gentian. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Centropogon amplicorollinus include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Centropogon amplicorollinus is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are valued for their potential health benefits and traditional uses.
